Typo – Please note the following authoritative statement: "It was spelled "mungoose" in the 18th and 19th centuries." However, the spelling in Current Times is now "mongoose".  Therefore this may be considered as a Global Change, so that Users may receive "Modern Day" English Words, rather than 18th Century terminology.

Typo – This is a possible "Print Change" that needs investigation. It appears that Three Genders (masculine, neuter and feminine) are being cited for this definition: "a sort of grass". Therefore, "kaseruka" is likely intended as the Neuter Form, which of course should be spelled as "kaserukam".

Typo – This is a possible "Print Change" that needs investigation. It appears that Three Genders (masculine, neuter and feminine) are being cited for this definition: "a sort of grass". Therefore, "kaserukāḥ" is likely intended as the Feminine Form, which of course should be spelled as "kaserukā". It appears that VCP, SKD and SHS agree. MW also indicates this correction under His Headword "kaśeruka".
